So this guy, he's got pink phone message slips in piles on the floor with bricks on top of him. Red brick, white brick, blue brick. And I said, what's the deal with the bricks, right? And he says, well, you know, we got a lot of leads. And I get back to him as best I can. He said, so the ones under the red brick, they've called at least three times. The ones under the blue bricks, they've called twice. And the ones under the white bricks, we think have only called in once. And he said, I'm never going to get to the ones under the white brick until they move to the red brick. He said, I'm doing the best I can. I mean, there were leads everywhere. And this was the system. I might add, by the way, these guys are in the computer business. But there was an organized chaos to all of this. That was impressive. And when you hear some of the numbers, and translate them to today's revenue, you'll be very impressed. And amongst other things, which is where I want to get to tonight, all of these sales occurred. There was a timing issue, and it was the right thing at the right time and all that. But they really mostly occurred on the strength of George communicating with the prospects in print and on audio tape. And he is one of those guys that is not only deliberate and smart about it, but there's then an intangible, so you watch Michael Jordan play basketball, and you can see some mechanical things, but you can't really explain the entire performance by mechanical things. Top golfers, same way. Top honors racing drivers, same way. There's a notch above the conscious, deliberate, knowledgeable how to. There's something else they do. And so he has that sort of this instinctive, intuitive connection with the people who bought these opportunities. And so I want to get there. But you don't very often have, look, most of the guys from this particular golden era of the opportunity business are already dead. So he's stubbornly hanging around. So you don't very often get an opportunity. If you went back and looked at the big businesses and the ads, there's a few, I'll show you one tomorrow, that's still here and still cranking. But of that era, most of these guys, you can't pick their brains because they're either dead or the brains are gone and the body's still sitting in a chair somewhere. So I was always impressed. Halbert, you could pick Halbert copy out if you knew Halbert and you would look at it. I remember he, Ron LeGrand brought to a Platinum meeting. He had Halbert do an ad for him and he had four other ads. And if you didn't know, pick the best ad. Nobody would pick the Halbert ad. Everybody would pick the other ads. The only reason I was right that day is because I recognized it as a Halbert ad. And I can't tell you why it would out, because if I hadn't, I would pick one of the other ones too. But Halbert could connect with every man in a way that transcended mechanics. And George could connect with people considering opportunity in a way that transcends mechanics. So we'll get there tonight, but I want to lay a little background so you have a sense of where all this came from. And then it's really up to you guys to pick brains, not me. So I'm only going to go so far and then I'm going to let you have at it. And you should know George has made it very clear he will answer anything. So you can ask anything that you like. So with that said, and I'll tell you what, I changed my mind. That's unusual. Yeah, so before we do the business stuff, I want to tell the heart story. So I changed my mind. So you should know you're looking at a dead guy. I know he's moving, but he's dead. So start with being dead and tell me how George got a heart story. Do you need a prompt? No, I just want you to understand my wife told me never to tell her. Well, you don't have to tell how you died. Oh, you got to do it. I can't tell it without. I was making love to my wife, reached a climax and died on top of her. It's a good way to start a story. It's a good way to start a story. My wife is the smartest woman I've ever met in my life. I started dating her when she was 15. I was 16. I've never had another girlfriend. Never. I don't know where it is. I just have one. Ball. And it's shameless to use that. Shameless? What's that mean? But it's effective. It's effective. Oh, you got them all on your side now, say. Oh, I didn't know that. What am I talking about? You dropped dead. Thank you. There you go. So she has been trained over our lifetime to handle anything. And so when I died on top of her, she picked up the telephone, called 911 or whatever number you call it, it's 911, and told them what happened. And they said, do you do CPR? And she said, no, it's all right, you do what we tell you to. And so she said, I did what they told me to. And within six, I live in the country, I live on a farm. And I've lived a long way from town. And they got there in five and a half minutes. I was dead, but they couldn't revive me. They tell me, you probably know more than I do about it. About six minutes, and that's all there is to it. So they got there in five and a half minutes, put a defibrillator on me, and got me going.
